blocking_summary: Missing specialist security stage artifacts (security_report.md + security_result.json) required by plan. No explicit waiver recorded. Per plans/deferred-cleanup-plan.md, the Coordinator must not record this subtask complete without them.
test_sufficiency_summary: Sufficient for stated acceptance criteria. Paired accept/reject-class tests for event-handler and data: URI suites correctly demonstrate both blocking and acceptance behavior. 14 controller tests pass with no regression. One gap: HTML comment bypass (W1) not documented by test.
documentation_accuracy_summary: docs/features/media.md accurately reflects the implemented six rejection classes, anchoring policy, and prose-acceptance examples. No contradiction with in-code JSDoc.

findings.summary: Specialist security stage artifacts absent; no explicit waiver recorded
findings.detail: plans/deferred-cleanup-plan.md subtask-8 requires security_report.md + security_result.json covering the entire apps/api/src/media/ subsystem. Neither file exists in the artifact directory and no explicit waiver has been recorded.

findings.summary: HTML comment bypass in event-handler pattern — low real-world risk
findings.detail: Input ' onload=x>' bypasses /<[^>]*\bon\w+\s*=/i because [^>]* stops at '>' in '-->'. No major browser executes event handlers on such malformed tags. Client-side MarkdownRenderer strips all raw HTML as defense-in-depth.

findings.summary: Controller test count discrepancy in acceptance criteria statement
findings.detail: Acceptance criteria states '11 existing controller tests pass' but the file contains 14 it() cases, all passing. Count discrepancy is in task description only; actual coverage is broader.

findings.summary: Tab/newline mid-word in 'javascript:' not caught — acceptable tolerance
findings.detail: 'javas\tcript:' bypasses /javascript\s*:/i. Not a real browser bypass vector.

findings.summary: javascript: and vbscript: patterns are context-free — intentional, documented
findings.detail: Implementer reviewed and accepted these as context-free; false-positive risk is negligible. Decision documented in implementer report.
Known limitation of regex-based sanitization. Mitigated by client-side MarkdownRenderer URI scheme re-validation.
